alerts.title: Iran nuclear breakout window narrows to 1-2 weeks amid stalled talks
alerts.summary: Global metrics confirm Iran's uranium enrichment remains at 60% U-235 with estimated breakout time of ~1-2 weeks. Despite 60-day US Treasury sanctions waiver (effective until 21 August 2026) and ongoing US-Iran negotiations, technical work on dilution and stockpile disposal remains incomplete. Heightened risk of unilateral Iranian acceleration if diplomatic momentum stalls further.

alerts.title: Trump threatens immediate Iran strikes; negotiations pause amid escalation rhetoric
alerts.summary: Incidents feed reports Trump administration issuing explicit threats to 'hit Iran very hard tonight' and seize oil/gas sectors, causing scheduled US-Iran talks in Switzerland to pause. Multiple outlets (NPR, Fortune, Radio Free Europe) confirm threats precede dialogue, destabilizing fragile ceasefire and raising risk of accidental escalation during sensitive nuclear negotiations.

alerts.title: Four US carriers deployed across critical zones; largest naval concentration since 2022
alerts.summary: Assets feed confirms USS Dwight D. Eisenhower (Red Sea, 15.78°N 42.22°E), USS Abraham Lincoln (Arabian Sea), USS Gerald R. Ford (Mediterranean), and USS Theodore Roosevelt (Pacific) all simultaneously deployed alongside three Red Sea destroyers (Carney, Mason, Laboon) and amphibious assets. Positioning directly correlates with Houthi 'total ban' declarations on Israeli shipping and Iranian missile bombardment warnings.
alerts.region: Red Sea / Arabian Sea / Persian Gulf / Mediterranean

alerts.title: Houthis escalate maritime blockade; declare 'total ban' on Israeli vessels in Red Sea
alerts.summary: Multiple Telegram sources (Long War Journal, Reuters, Al Arabiya, Anadolu Agency) confirm Houthis announced total maritime ban on Israeli shipping in Red Sea effective immediately. Recent drone and missile attacks on Israel coordinate with declaration, signaling coordinated proxy escalation tied to Iran-Israel ceasefire collapse and Trump's renewed threat rhetoric.

alerts.title: US negative sentiment spikes to 52%; Trump-Iran tensions dominate discourse
alerts.summary: Sentiment analysis shows US negative sentiment at 52% (up from recent baseline), with 'Trump' (69 mentions), 'Iran' (30 mentions), and 'judge' (20 mentions) dominating word cloud. Surge reflects combination of Trump's explicit strike threats, ongoing negotiations, and legal/political controversy, signaling polarization and public anxiety over imminent regional conflict.

analysis_note: Regional escalation cycle accelerates as Trump administration combines explicit military threats with nuclear diplomacy, narrowing Iran's breakout window to 1-2 weeks while US naval force reaches four-carrier deployment; proxy actors (Houthis, Hezbollah) simultaneously escalate amid fragile ceasefire collapse.
